LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DINESH MANIAR'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFFS JAMES ZANZE, LISA ZANZE, AND MARK ARMENANTE - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. WHILE PLAINTIFFS HAVE A PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION AS PROVIDED BY SECTION 827 OF THE SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC WORKS CODE, PLAINTIFFS DID NOT ALLEGE HOW THE ASSERTED VIOLATION OF ARTICLE 16.1 CAUSED THEM HARM OR WHAT RELIEF THEY SEEK FOR THE ASSERTED VIOLATION. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT DINESH MANIAR'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFFS JAMES ZANZE, LISA ZANZE, AND MARK ARMENANTE.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action in the first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend. While plaintiffs have a private right of action as provided by section 827 of the San Francisco Public Works Code, plaintiffs did not allege how the asserted violation of article 16.1 caused them harm or what relief they seek for the asserted violation. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
